about.intro: Hi, I'm Anastasiya - originally from Minsk, now based in Warsaw.
about.p1: Art has been part of my life for literally as long as I can remember. I started drawing when I was around three years old, graduated from an art college in Minsk, and later continued my education at the Academy of Fine Arts in Wrocław.
about.p2: Creativity has always been my language - not only tattooing, but everything connected to visual expression, aesthetics, emotions, stories, and human connection. I genuinely love creating, and I equally love meeting people through this work.
about.p3: For me, tattooing is never just about "getting a tattoo." I approach every client individually, because every person, story, and idea is different. The process of creating something together - something personal, meaningful, and beautiful - is one of the most special parts of this job for me.
about.p4: I really value the trust people give me when they choose me for something so personal and permanent. It's always a unique experience, not only artistically, but emotionally too. And honestly, being part of that means a lot to me.
about.ps: P.S. I also have two dogs that I'm completely obsessed with, so there's a very high chance you'll see photos of them during our session.

contacts.label: CONTACT
contacts.title: Booking & consultations.
contacts.description: For a quick quote, send: 2–5 references, placement, approximate size (cm) and preferred dates. I reply within 24–48h.
contacts.preferDm: Prefer DM? Instagram is the fastest.

0.question: Does it hurt?
0.answer: Pain depends on the placement and your personal tolerance, but I always try to make the session as calm and comfortable as possible. Breaks are always welcome.

1.question: How much does a tattoo cost?
1.answer:
Pricing depends on the size, placement, level of detail, and session length.
The minimum price starts from €150.

2.question: Is a deposit required?
2.answer: Yes. A deposit (€100-€200) is required to secure your appointment and is deducted from the final price.

3.question: Can I bring my own idea or references?
3.answer: Of course. References, moodboards, sketches, or even vague ideas are always welcome. I use them to create a custom design that fits your body and personal aesthetic.

4.question: How do I prepare for a session?
4.answer: Get enough sleep, eat well before the appointment, stay hydrated, and avoid alcohol the day before. Comfortable clothing is always a good idea.

5.question: How long does the healing take?
5.answer: The surface usually heals within 2-3 weeks, while full healing can take a bit longer depending on the tattoo and your skin. Proper aftercare makes a big difference.

6.question: Do you create custom designs?
6.answer: Always. Every tattoo is designed individually for the client - I don't copy other artists' work.

finalCta.title: Ready for your next piece?
finalCta.description: Send your idea, placement and approximate size — I'll reply within 24–48h with a clear quote and available dates.

pricing.depositNote: Required to secure your appointment and deducted from the final price.
pricing.quoteNote: For an exact quote, send your idea, references, placement, approximate size, and preferred dates.
pricing.roughIdea: You don't need a fully formed idea - even a rough concept or mood is enough to start.

0.title: Send your idea
0.description:
Send me your tattoo idea, inspiration images, references, or simply the mood and feeling you want the tattoo to have.

Even if the concept is still rough or unfinished - that’s completely okay.

1.title: Placement & size
1.description: Please include the placement on the body, approximate size in centimeters, and a photo of the area where you’d like the tattoo.

2.title: Preferred month
2.description: Let me know which month you’d like to book your session for, so we can find a suitable date together.

3.title: Design & booking
3.description:
After receiving all the information, I prepare a custom sketch for you.

We discuss the final details, confirm the appointment date, and talk through the pricing.

4.title: Session day
4.description: Then we meet in the studio and create your new beautiful tattoo :)

reviews.note: Real client feedback. More highlights on Instagram.

0.text: The cleanest linework I've seen. Calm session, great guidance, and the result looks exactly like the sketch.
0.author: Anna

1.text: Professional from start to finish. Sterility, attention to detail — and a very clean healing.
1.author: Kira

2.text: Loved the composition and how it fits my body. Minimal, elegant, and done with real precision.
2.author: Sofia

0.title: Sterile, always
0.text:
Professional hygiene standards are a priority in every session.

Single-use needles and disposable supplies only, with a fully cleaned and disinfected workspace before and after each appointment.

1.title: Custom design
1.text:
Every tattoo is created individually for the client and placement.

I focus on body flow, composition, and details to make each piece feel natural, balanced, and personal - never copied or generic.

2.title: Clean lines
2.text:
Precision matters to me.

I pay close attention to line quality, contrast, and readability so the tattoo heals beautifully and stays visually strong over time.

3.title: Smooth booking
3.text:
Easy communication, quick replies, and a transparent process from start to finish.

We discuss your idea, placement, size, and pricing beforehand so everything feels clear, organized, and stress-free.

4.title: Comfortable sessions
4.text:
I want the experience to feel calm, safe, and human.

Comfortable atmosphere, good music, soulful conversations, breaks when needed - and no pressure to survive the session like a medieval warrior.

aftercare.heroSubtitle: Proper healing is part of the art.

first24h.0: Keep the bandage on for 2–4 hours (unless advised otherwise).
first24h.1: Wash gently with lukewarm water and fragrance-free soap.
first24h.2: Pat dry with a clean towel — do not rub.
first24h.3: Apply a very thin layer of recommended aftercare cream.

days2to14.1: Apply a thin layer of cream as needed.
days2to14.2: Avoid tight clothing on the tattooed area.
days2to14.3: Do not scratch or pick peeling skin.

aftercare.note: Healing usually takes 2–4 weeks depending on placement and skin type. If you notice unusual redness, swelling, or irritation — contact me immediately.
